target.profile_backing: criterion target eval/add_64k_f64_vec from fj-lax lax_baseline after pass43 left the residual large-F64 elementwise storage gap as the top no-gaps mcqr.30 target

one_lever.name: dense F64 LiteralBuffer storage for Value::vector_f64
one_lever.description: TensorValue F64 vectors now use a dense Arc<Vec<f64>> LiteralBuffer representation with lazy public Literal materialization. The current same-shape F64 arithmetic route consumes LiteralBuffer::as_f64_slice and returns TensorValue::new_f64_values, so large F64 Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Max/Min avoid the 24-byte Literal input representation while preserving the existing public Literal APIs.

baseline.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-pass44-baseline-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/add_64k_f64' --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-pass44-after-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/add_64k_f64' --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Dense same-shape arithmetic zips lhs and rhs f64 slices in the same row-major element order as the previous Literal loop and collects outputs in that order.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: No sorting, dispatch priority, or comparison key changed. Max and Min still call the existing jax_max_f64 and jax_min_f64 helpers, so NaN propagation and signed-zero behavior match the scalar path.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Each element uses the same scalar f64 closure (+, -, *, /, jax_max_f64, jax_min_f64) as before. No reductions, reassociation, fused multiply-add, approximate math, BLAS, LAPACK, MKL, XLA, unsafe code, or cross-element SIMD reordering was introduced.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG sources, seeds, sampling order, or nondeterministic iteration paths changed.
isomorphism_proof.public_literal_api: LiteralBuffer::as_slice, Deref, AsRef, Index, IntoIterator, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, PartialEq, and to_vec expose the same Literal sequence as the previous Vec<Literal> representation. Dense storage materializes through Literal::from_f64, preserving f64 bit payloads, signed zero, and NaN payloads.
isomorphism_proof.mutation_aliasing: Mutable operations and mutable indexing materialize dense storage to Literal storage before mutation, preserving clone-by-value observable behavior.
isomorphism_proof.malformed_tensors: TensorValue::new remains element-count-only for declared F64 tensors with malformed Literal payloads. Dense storage is used only by the new TensorValue::new_f64_values constructor and Value::vector_f64, so malformed literal-backed tensors still fall back to the old path.

notes: The after dense_ref row is a bench-local raw Vec clone/add reference and does not exercise eval_primitive or production TensorValue storage. It was noisy on vmi1264463 and is not used as the keep/drop target.
The production target row is profile-backed and improved by 2.6452x.
No C BLAS, MKL, LAPACK, XLA, unsafe code, or behavior-altering floating-point reassociation was introduced.
